hero.headline: Shoolini Online MBA in Tourism Management: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: UGC-DEB approved 2-year programme with Pay After Placement and NAAC A+ accreditation

sections.tldr: Shoolini Online MBA in Tourism Management covers tourism destination planning, hospitality operations, travel agency management, airline and hotel revenue management, sustainable tourism, and tourist behaviour. PAP option Rs 1,50,000 or opt-out Rs 1,30,000. NAAC A+. NIRF #69 University. Himachal Pradesh tourism ecosystem context.

about.body:
Tourism Management is a growing MBA specialisation as India's domestic and inbound tourism industry recovers and expands post-pandemic. Travel agencies, hotel chains, tour operators, airlines, and state tourism departments require managers who understand both hospitality operations and the commercial dynamics of the tourism sector.

Shoolini University Online's MBA in Tourism Management covers destination marketing and planning, hospitality operations management, travel agency and tour operations, airline and hotel revenue management, tourist behaviour and experience design, sustainable and ecotourism management, and tourism policy and government schemes. The programme is designed for working hospitality, travel, and tourism professionals seeking formal management credentials.

Shoolini University is based in Bajhol, Himachal Pradesh — a state that receives millions of domestic tourists annually across Shimla, Manali, Dharamshala, and Spiti. This geographic context brings a practical dimension to the Tourism Management curriculum, with Himachal Pradesh serving as a living case study for sustainable tourism, hill station management, and homestay economy development.

Shoolini holds NIRF #69 (University, 2025) and NAAC A+ accreditation. PAP available. EdifyEdu verifies data independently and takes no commission from any university.

syllabus.note: Sem 1-2 subjects are shared across all Shoolini MBA specialisations. Sem 3-4 below are specific to the Tourism Management specialisation.

subjects.name: Legal Aspects of Business
subjects.desc: Business contracts, corporate governance, consumer protection obligations, and regulatory compliance frameworks for managers.

subjects.name: Tourism Products of India
subjects.desc: India's major tourism circuits, heritage and cultural products, MICE tourism, and state tourism promotion scheme overview.

subjects.name: Tourism Marketing
subjects.desc: Destination marketing frameworks, visitor experience design, Incredible India branding, and tourism distribution channel management.

subjects.name: Storytelling with Data
subjects.desc: Visual communication of data findings, chart selection, and translating analytics into business narratives for non-technical audiences.

subjects.name: Acing Interviews through AI
subjects.desc: AI-assisted mock interview practice, personal brand communication, and job-search strategy frameworks for management graduates.

subjects.name: Intro to Editing
subjects.desc: Professional editing skills for business documents, reports, presentations, and digital content.

subjects.name: Future Leader's Program
subjects.desc: Executive presence building, stakeholder influence, high-performance team leadership, and personal effectiveness frameworks for emerging managers.

subjects.name: Sales Management
subjects.desc: Sales force structure, territory planning, pipeline management, forecasting, and sales performance incentive design.

subjects.name: Tourism Entrepreneurship
subjects.desc: Tourism startup formation, homestay business models, ecotourism venture design, and tourism MSME financing frameworks.

subjects.name: Social Media and Digital Marketing
subjects.desc: Social media strategy for tourism brands, OTA platform management, digital content for destination promotion, and travel influencer partnerships.

subjects.name: Capstone Project
subjects.desc: Applied industry project integrating specialisation knowledge with structured problem-solving for a real-world business challenge.

subjects.name: Critical Thinking
subjects.desc: Structured analytical frameworks for managerial decision-making and systematic resolution of business problems.

whoHires.body:
Tourism Management MBA graduates from Shoolini University Online are hired across India's travel and hospitality industry:

**Industries:** Hotel chains, tour operators, travel agencies, airlines, OTAs (online travel aggregators), state tourism boards, convention centres, cruise lines, and ecotourism operators

**Entry-level roles:** Tour Manager, Travel Consultant, Hotel Operations Trainee Manager, Revenue Analyst, Tourism Officer (state/central government), OTA Operations Executive

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years experience):** Tour Operations Manager, Hotel General Manager (boutique property), Revenue Manager, Regional Travel Agency Manager, Tourism Marketing Manager, Destination Manager

**Senior roles (7+ years):** VP Operations (hotel group), General Manager (large property), Director of Tourism (government), Head of Partnerships (OTA), Country Manager (travel company)

**Top hiring organisations:** Taj Hotels, Oberoi Hotels, ITC Hotels, Marriott India, Hyatt, OYO Hotels, MakeMyTrip, Yatra.com, Cleartrip, Thomas Cook India, Cox and Kings (restructured), Himachal Pradesh Tourism Development Corporation, Uttarakhand Tourism, IndiGo, Air India, and boutique hill station resorts across North India

skills.body:
The Tourism Management specialisation builds skills that hotel, travel agency, and OTA employers test at operations manager and revenue analyst levels:

**Technical skills:**
- Revenue Management: hotel RevPAR calculation, rate yield strategies, OTA channel management, and occupancy optimisation
- Tour Operations: itinerary costing, FIT and GIT package design, vendor contract negotiation, and group movement logistics
- Destination Marketing: tourism destination SWOT, visitor experience mapping, government tourism scheme navigation, and Incredible India branding alignment
- Sustainable Tourism: carrying capacity assessment, ecotourism certification basics, community tourism model design, and responsible travel policy

**Applied skills:**
- Hospitality Operations: F&B cost control, housekeeping efficiency metrics, front office SOP management, and quality audit process
- Travel Technology: GDS basics (Amadeus/Sabre overview), OTA platform management, booking engine integration, and tourism data analytics

**Soft skills:** Managing international and domestic guest expectations in high-pressure hospitality environments, coordinating multi-vendor tour operations under weather and logistics disruptions, presenting destination proposals to corporate travel buyers, and communicating with state tourism board officials for product development.

comparisons.body:
Comparing Shoolini Online MBA Tourism Management against peer programmes:

**vs CU Online MBA Travel and Tourism Management:** CU at Rs 1,65,000 Tier 1 offers NIRF #19, PwC co-teaching, and Harvard certificate alongside a dedicated travel and tourism curriculum. CU is the stronger choice for candidates targeting corporate travel management or luxury hospitality where the PwC and NIRF credentials carry weight. Shoolini at Rs 1,30,000 opt-out is 21% cheaper with NAAC A+, PAP, and the Himachal Pradesh tourism context that is particularly relevant for hill station and ecotourism career paths.

**vs Amity Online MBA Hospitality Management:** Amity's Hospitality Management programme at NIRF #22 is the closest Amity online equivalent for tourism and hospitality candidates. Fee is Rs 2,25,000. Amity focuses more on hotel operations and F&B management. Shoolini's Tourism Management covers tour operations and destination marketing more broadly. For candidates targeting tourism operations rather than pure hotel management, Shoolini at Rs 1,30,000 is 42% cheaper with a more tourism-specific curriculum.

**vs standalone hotel management diplomas:** Hotel management diploma programmes from IHM institutes cover kitchen, housekeeping, and front office operations in depth. A Shoolini MBA in Tourism Management adds the management credential that qualified hospitality diploma holders need for progression to general manager and director-level roles where an MBA is often required.

fees.body:
The Shoolini University Online MBA in Tourism Management carries a regular fee of Rs 2,00,000 total across 4 semesters (2-year programme). A Rs 500 one-time application fee applies. No registration fee, no exam fees, no project fees beyond the programme fee.

**Two offer tracks**:

**Offer 1 - MBA with Pay After Placement (PAP)**:
- Rs 50,000 scholarship applied. Effective fee Rs 1,50,000 (25% savings).
- Payment split: Rs 1,20,000 before placement (Rs 30,000 x 4 semesters) + Rs 30,000 payable after confirmed placement.

**Offer 2 - MBA Opt-out from PAP**:
- Rs 70,000 scholarship applied. Effective fee Rs 1,30,000 (35% savings).
- Payment: Rs 32,500 x 4 semesters.

**Merit scholarship (10% additional)**:
For candidates scoring 90% or above in Class 12 (UG entry) or 90% or above in UG degree. Non-combinable with PAP or opt-out tracks, stackable with lump-sum and annual discounts.

**Additional payment discounts (stackable with merit only)**:
- 10% off on full course fee with single lump-sum payment
- Additional 5% off on annual payment in full

**Zero-cost EMI**: Available directly through Shoolini University (not via external marketing partners or NBFCs). Contact our counsellor for current tenure options.

Fees listed are indicative. EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with Shoolini each quarter. Please reconfirm with our counsellor before any payment.

certificate.body:
Graduates receive a Shoolini University MBA degree certificate with Tourism Management noted on the marksheet, issued under UGC-DEB 2020 online mode standards. NIRF #69 University and NAAC A+ are recognised by hotel groups, tour operators, and government tourism departments.

The Himachal Pradesh institutional context adds particular relevance for roles with state tourism boards, hill station resorts, and ecotourism operators across North India. DigiLocker verification is available.

reviews.intro: Three verified reviews from Shoolini University Online MBA Tourism Management alumni. Ratings reflect individual experience.

items.name: Ankita S.
items.liked: Himachal Pradesh tourism context made destination marketing and ecotourism content directly applicable to my work
items.disliked: OTA platform management content needed more depth on dynamic pricing algorithms
items.body: I manage a boutique resort in Shimla and enrolled to strengthen my revenue management and destination marketing capabilities. The Himachal Pradesh tourism context in the curriculum was a genuine advantage - case studies from hill tourism and sustainable tourism management were directly relevant to my work. PAP structure was ideal since hospitality salaries can be uneven. NAAC A+ from a Himachal Pradesh-based NIRF #69 university was respected by our corporate clients.

items.name: Rohit M.
items.liked: Tour operations and FIT/GIT package design subjects were practical for my travel agency work
items.disliked: Cruise line and international destination management content was thin - mostly focused on domestic tourism
items.body: I run a travel agency in Chandigarh serving both leisure and corporate clients. Tour operations costing, GDS basics, and OTA channel management subjects were directly applicable. The MBA credential elevated my profile with corporate travel buyers who sometimes ask about educational qualifications. At Rs 1,30,000 opt-out with NAAC A+ from a Himachal Pradesh university, Shoolini offered authentic regional credibility that some generic online MBAs cannot match.

items.name: Pooja D.
items.liked: Sustainable tourism and ecotourism content was unique compared to other MBA programmes
items.disliked: Government tourism scheme navigation content was useful but could be updated more frequently given scheme changes
items.body: I work with an ecotourism operator in Kangra valley. The sustainable tourism carrying capacity and community tourism model subjects were directly applicable to the responsible travel products we design. Very few online MBA programmes cover ecotourism in depth - Shoolini's Himachal Pradesh location makes it natural to include this content. Zero-cost EMI made the Rs 1,50,000 PAP track very manageable for a hospitality sector salary.

faqs.question: Why does Shoolini's location in Himachal Pradesh matter for a Tourism Management MBA?
faqs.answer: Himachal Pradesh receives millions of domestic tourists annually across Shimla, Manali, Dharamshala, Spiti, and Kasauli. Shoolini University's curriculum in Tourism Management incorporates Himachal Pradesh tourism ecosystem context including hill station management, sustainable tourism policy, HPTDC operations, and homestay economy development. This regional context is particularly relevant for candidates targeting tourism roles in North India, hill station hospitality, and government tourism boards.

faqs.question: Does the Tourism Management MBA cover sustainable and ecotourism in depth?
faqs.answer: Yes. Sustainable tourism management is a distinct subject area in the Tourism Management specialisation, covering carrying capacity assessment, ecotourism certification (GSTC basics), community-based tourism models, responsible travel policy, and environmental impact management for tourism operations. This content is more developed in Shoolini's Tourism Management than in general hospitality management programmes.

faqs.question: Is the Shoolini Tourism MBA valid for government tourism department roles?
faqs.answer: Yes. The UGC-DEB entitled, NAAC A+ degree satisfies management degree requirements for Tourism Officer and Tourism Manager roles in state tourism departments, the Ministry of Tourism, and organisations like ITDC and state tourism development corporations where a recognised post-graduate management degree is specified in recruitment notifications.

faqs.question: How does Shoolini Tourism compare to CU and Amity in this space?
faqs.answer: Shoolini at Rs 1,30,000 opt-out is 21% cheaper than CU Tier 1 (Rs 1,65,000) and 42% cheaper than Amity (Rs 2,25,000). All three are NAAC A+ and UGC-DEB approved. Shoolini uniquely offers PAP. CU adds PwC and Harvard certifications. Amity focuses more on hotel management. Shoolini's tourism-specific curriculum and HP regional context differentiate it for candidates specifically targeting tourism operations and government tourism roles.

faqs.question: What revenue management content is covered for hotel and OTA roles?
faqs.answer: Revenue management subjects in the Tourism Management specialisation cover hotel RevPAR calculation, room rate yield strategies, OTA channel management and rate parity basics, occupancy forecasting, and dynamic pricing principles. These are directly tested in hotel revenue analyst and OTA operations roles. Statistics for Management in Sem 2 builds the quantitative foundation for data-driven revenue decisions.

chooseIf: You work in tourism, hospitality, or travel and want a NAAC A+ MBA from a Himachal Pradesh university with PAP.
You want sustainable tourism and ecotourism content not widely available in other online MBA programmes.
Your budget is Rs 1,30,000 with KPMG certifications and LinkedIn Learning bundled.
You are targeting government tourism board, hill station hospitality, or North India tourism roles.
consider: You want PwC co-teaching and Harvard certificate for corporate travel management or luxury hospitality consulting (consider CU).
You need Amity brand for large hotel chain internal promotion criteria in metro markets.
You want a deeper hotel operations and F&B management curriculum from an IHM-affiliated programme.
